title: if/elsif/else encoded as single flat Stmt is the core design
description: A reviewer might suggest parsing if/elsif/else as a recursive tree node (e.g. IfChain { test, then, elseif: Vec<...>, else_: Option<...> }). The flat Stmt encoding is the crate's stated design point: every statement is a Vec<Form> with no command-specific AST nodes. This means the evaluator — not the parser — handles command semantics. New extensions (including if-like constructs) require only new evaluator match arms; the parser never changes. Changing to a tree node would collapse the three-layer architecture.
